macro_segments.title: Introduction to Mishna 25a: Categories of Disqualified Witnesses and Judges

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and overview of Mishna on Sanhedrin 25a
Rashi's introduction: all disqualified persons are like thieves; basis in Torah
List of disqualified persons: dice players, lenders on interest, pigeon flyers, Shviit traders
Rabbi Yehuda's qualification: disqualification applies only without other trade

macro_segments.title: The Dice Player (Mesachek B'Kubia): Asmakhta Doctrine and Productive Activity

micro_segments.title: Rami bar Chama: gambling is asmakhta (invalid stipulation); money transfer not real
Rav Sheshet: gambling is not asmakhta; disqualification based on non-productive activity
Difference between opinions: application if gambler has another trade
Challenge to Rami bar Chama from Mishna: Rabbi Yehuda says only without other trade
Resolution: amatai (when) indicates clarification, not disagreement; all agree on reason
Beraita from Tarfon on nezirut: conditional oaths without explicit declaration are invalid
Beraita supports Rami bar Chama: asmakhta doctrine applies to uncertain outcomes

macro_segments.title: Lender on Interest (Malveh B'Ribit): Who Is Disqualified and Testimony Standards

micro_segments.title: Rava: borrower who takes loan on interest is disqualified from testimony
Case of Bar Binyatus: two witnesses testify he loaned on interest; Rava disqualifies
Resolution: person cannot disqualify himself; testimony against another remains valid

macro_segments.title: The Dishonest Shochet (Ritual Slaughterer) and Repentance Conditions

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman disqualifies shochet caught selling non-kosher animals; shochet attempts contrition
Rava: subterfuge; true repentance requires moving to unknown place and proving changed behavior

macro_segments.title: Pigeon Flyers and Animal Fighters: Arah vs. Pigeon Racing Interpretations

micro_segments.title: Definition: mafrichei yonim refers either to pigeon racing or training pigeons to steal
Rabbi Chama bar Oshaya: arah (trained bird stealer) depends on darchei shalom
Resolution: two views explain same phenomenon; both mentions necessary for different reasons
Dice players depend on own skill; pigeon racing depends on animal; both cause non-relinquishment
Baraita: categories expanded to animal fighting; repentance requires breaking tools and ceasing
Rashi's note: repentance clauses added by Amoraic sages, not original Baraita

macro_segments.title: Shviit Traders and Tax Collectors: Extended Disqualifications from Baraita

micro_segments.title: Shviit traders disqualified; repentance requires waiting for next Shviit year and returning profits
Rabbi Nechemia: repentance requires not just behavioral change but monetary restitution
Thieves and chasmans (violent takers) added to disqualified list; basis in unscrupulousness
Finding rule: stealing from cheresh, shoteh, katan only derabbanan; mipnei darchei shalom
Shepherds added to disqualified: originally thought accidental; later saw intentional field invasion
Tax collectors: originally trusted to take only assessed amount; later seen taking excess

macro_segments.title: Shepherds in Eretz Yisrael vs. Diaspora: Size of Herd and Disqualification Standards

micro_segments.title: Rava: disqualification applies to both small and large animal shepherds equally
Contradiction: Rava elsewhere says small animal shepherds disqualified only in EY, kosher outside
Resolution: second statement refers to animals raised domestically, not pastured in field
Rashi: strictness in EY due to yishuv EY; preventing field invasion improves settlement
Support from Mishna: three trusted cow herders implies technical disqualification as judges
Three herders are trustworthy judges despite being ordinarily disqualified

macro_segments.title: Default Status of Shepherds vs. Tax Collectors: Rebuttable Presumption

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda: random shepherd presumed disqualified; random tax collector presumed valid

topical_tags.term: adam karov atzmo (person is considered close to himself)
Eretz Yisrael vs. chutz la-aretz (Israel vs. diaspora)
